title: Amendment of section 5 of the Computer Crimes Act 1997
verbatim:
The Computer Crimes Act 1997 [Act 563] is amended in section 5 by substituting for subsection (2) the following subsection:
'(2) If the offence is committed against any national critical information infrastructure as defined in the Cyber Security Act 2024 [Act 854], the offender shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding one million ringgit or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding fifteen years or to both.'
translation: Amends the Computer Crimes Act 1997 s5 (unauthorized access to computer material) to create an enhanced penalty where the offence targets NCII: RM1,000,000 and/or 15 years imprisonment (significantly higher than the standard penalty). This aligns the criminal law framework with NCII protection priorities.

title: Amendment of section 6 of the Computer Crimes Act 1997
verbatim:
The Computer Crimes Act 1997 is amended in section 6 by substituting for subsection (2) the following subsection:
'(2) If the offence is committed against any national critical information infrastructure as defined in the Cyber Security Act 2024, the offender shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding one million ringgit or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding fifteen years or to both.'
translation: Amends the Computer Crimes Act 1997 s6 (unauthorized access with intent to commit or facilitate commission of further offence) to create enhanced penalties when the offence affects NCII: RM1,000,000 and/or 15 years. Targets escalated intrusion scenarios and exploitation of access to NCII to commit further crimes.

title: Amendment of section 7 of the Computer Crimes Act 1997
verbatim:
The Computer Crimes Act 1997 is amended in section 7 by substituting for subsection (2) the following subsection:
'(2) If the offence is committed against any national critical information infrastructure as defined in the Cyber Security Act 2024, the offender shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding one million five hundred thousand ringgit or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding twenty years or to both.'
translation: Amends the Computer Crimes Act 1997 s7 (unauthorized modification of the contents of any computer) with the highest enhanced penalty tier for NCII-related offences: RM1,500,000 and/or 20 years imprisonment. Reflects the severity of tampering with data or programs in critical national infrastructure systems.

title: New section 8A of the Computer Crimes Act 1997
verbatim:
The Computer Crimes Act 1997 is amended by inserting after section 8 the following section:
'8A. Cyber security offences against national critical information infrastructure
(1) Any person who knowingly causes or attempts to cause a cyber security incident against any national critical information infrastructure as defined in the Cyber Security Act 2024 commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding two million ringgit or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding twenty years or to both.
(2) For the purposes of this section, national critical information infrastructure has the meaning assigned to it in the Cyber Security Act 2024.'
translation: Inserts a new offence (s8A) into the Computer Crimes Act 1997: knowingly causing or attempting to cause a cyber security incident against NCII. This carries the heaviest penalties in the entire framework — RM2,000,000 and/or 20 years imprisonment. This creates a dedicated criminal offence for deliberate attacks on Malaysia's critical infrastructure, separate from general computer crimes.
